title.name: Standing Light Punch
title.notes: Chains into 5LP/2LP/2LK; SA2 cancel: +1/-5

title.name: Standing Medium Punch
title.notes: True blockstring when chained into 5MP~MK even at max delay; SA2 cancel: +5/+4

title.name: Standing Heavy Punch
title.notes: 1f extra recovery on whiff and block

title.name: Standing Light Kick
title.notes: DR cancel is delayed until after the 3rd active frame; SA2 cancel: +2/-3

title.name: Standing Medium Kick
title.notes: SA2 cancel: +9/+3

title.name: Standing Heavy Kick
title.notes: Super cancel is delayed until after active frames; SA2 cancel: +10/+4

title.name: Crouching Light Punch
title.notes: Chains into 5LP/2LP/2LK; DR cancel is delayed until after active frames; topmost hurtbox on frames 1-5 is vulnerable only to aerial strikes (preventing its use as an anti-air); SA2 cancel: +1/-4

title.name: Crouching Medium Punch
title.notes: 3f extra recovery on whiff; topmost hurtbox on frames 1-10 is vulnerable only to aerial strikes (preventing its use as an anti-air); SA2 cancel: +8/+1

title.name: Crouching Heavy Punch
title.notes: SA2 cancel: +10/+8

title.name: Crouching Light Kick
title.notes: Chains into 5LP/2LP/2LK

title.name: Crouching Medium Kick
title.notes: Special/DR cancel (but not DI cancel) is delayed until after active frames; SA2 cancel: +7/+0

title.name: Jumping Light Punch
title.notes: Fastest air-to-air; good hitbox priority

title.name: Jumping Medium Punch
title.notes: Puts airborne opponents into a limited juggle state; lower body projectile invuln 8-23f except around attack hitbox (cannot be used to avoid the 2nd hit of JP's OD Amnesia bombs)

title.name: Jumping Heavy Punch
title.notes: Spikes airborne opponents on counter-hit/Punish Counter; has juggle potential (causes air reset when juggled after 236HK); lower body projectile invuln 9-23f except around attack hitbox (cannot be used to avoid the 2nd hit of JP's OD Amnesia bombs); hitbox extends farther beyond Ingrid's arm (visually deceptive); best downward reach of Ingrid's jump-ins for vertical approaches

title.name: Jumping Light Kick
title.notes: Cross-up; good air-to-air for potential cross-ups (e.g. E. Honda Sumo Smash, M. Bison Devil Reverse)

title.name: Jumping Medium Kick
title.notes: Cross-up; lower body projectile invuln 8-21f (if used immediately on the way up, can avoid the 2nd hit of JP's OD Amnesia bombs)

title.name: Jumping Heavy Kick
title.notes: Puts airborne opponents into a limited juggle state (can juggle into Target Combo extension); lower body projectile invuln 12-19f except around attack hitbox (cannot be used to avoid the 2nd hit of JP's OD Amnesia bombs); best horizontal range of Ingrid's jump-ins

title.name: Sun Bright
title.notes: Very high active frame count for an overhead (up to +6 oH / 0 oB with meaty timing)

title.name: Halo Flight
title.notes: 3f extra recovery on whiff; cancelable into j.214P on hit or block (cancel is delayed until after 1st recovery frame); KD +10 after cancel into j.214LP; can lead to huge damage with Sun Crests and j.214PP hitconfirm; high pushback keeps it safe on block in most scenarios

title.name: Glowing Touch 1
title.notes: 6f blockstring gap into 4MK~HP TC (up to 8f if delayed)

title.name: Luminous Uppercut 1
title.notes: Whiffs on crouching opponents; slow but solid anti-air with an extremely rewarding Target Combo juggle follow-up; strong hitbox but no head or upper body invincibility (likely to trade if not timed early); cannot hit cross-up; very short horizontal range on grounded opponents; 7f blockstring gap into 4HP~HP TC (up to 11f if delayed)

title.name: Pretty Heel Kick
title.notes: True blockstring even at max delay; SA2 cancel: +9/+1

title.name: Glowing Touch 2
title.notes: 6f blockstring gap between hits (up to 8f if delayed)

title.name: Luminous Uppercut 2
title.notes: Whiffs on crouching opponents (opponent can crouch block even after stand blocking the initial 4HP); reduced KD Advantage if only the 2nd hit connects on a grounded opponent; 7f blockstring gap between hits (up to 11f if delayed); SA2 cancel: KD+53/+0

title.name: Satellite Leap
title.notes: Puts airborne opponents into a limited juggle state

title.name: Strange Knuckle
title.notes: No true throw loop, but can dash to become +1 for a corner pseudo-throw loop (also in range for a 2-hit light confirm); can set up a LP Sun Shot that allows Ingrid to dash in with frame advantage while recovering quickly enough to anti-air if the opponent jumps out of the corner; a successful Throw Escape builds 0.5 Drive bars for the defender; Throw Escape is awarded to a random player if input simultaneously

title.name: Gravity Drop
title.notes: Side switch; no true throw oki after corner side switch but can threaten with ranged normals, forward dash (+0), or Drive Rush normals; can set up a LP Sun Shot that allows Ingrid to dash in with frame advantage while recovering quickly enough to anti-air if the opponent jumps out of the corner; a successful Throw Escape builds 0.5 Drive bars for the defender; Throw Escape is awarded to a random player if input simultaneously

title.name: Twinkle Kick
title.notes: See [[Street_Fighter_6/Gauges#Drive_Impact|Drive Impact]]. Airborne connect gives spinning juggle state on Punish Counter or corner Wall Splat with variable height.

title.name: Odd Flight (Block)
title.notes: Performed by inputting 6HPHK during blockstun; 5f extra recovery on hit; freezes the screen for 4f during startup; see [[Street_Fighter_6/Gauges#Drive_Reversal|Drive Reversal]].

title.name: Odd Flight (Recovery)
title.notes: Performed by holding 6HPHK on wakeup; 5f extra recovery on hit; does not have any screen freeze except for a 12f freeze when interacting with the opponent's throw hitbox (this also refunds 1 Drive bar after punishing the throw); see [[Street_Fighter_6/Gauges#Drive_Reversal|Drive Reversal]].

title.name: Drive Parry
title.notes: See [[Street_Fighter_6/Gauges#Drive_Parry|Drive Parry]]. Perfect Parrying has only 1F of recovery and disables the opponent from canceling their attack. Perfect Parrying projectiles puts you into fixed 11 frames of recovery. After a Perfect Parry, all Super Gauge gain is reduced by 20% and Drive Gauge gain/damage is reduced by 50% for the entire punish combo.

title.name: Parry Drive Rush
title.notes: See [[Street_Fighter_6/Gauges#Drive_Rush|Drive Rush]]; 1-bar version performed out of a Parry; startup refers to minimum time before an attack can cancel the Drive Rush animation (if performed immediately after a successful Parry, only 1+8 startup); the first 15 recovery frames are cancelable into any attack; the final 22 recovery frames are cancelable into any attack or movement option or blocking (except Parry); only Normal and Command Normal attacks are enhanced with +4 frame advantage and improved juggle properties; Distance: 0.375 (min/throw), 1.502 (min/block), 3.083 (max/final DR frame)

title.name: Drive Rush Cancel
title.notes: See [[Street_Fighter_6/Gauges#Drive_Rush|Drive Rush]]; 3-bar version performed on hit/block from a cancelable normal; can also be performed on whiff from cancelable light normals; startup refers to minimum time before an attack can cancel the Drive Rush animation; the first 15 recovery frames are cancelable into any attack; the final 22 recovery frames are cancelable into any attack or movement option or blocking (except Parry); only Normal and Command Normal attacks are enhanced with +4 frame advantage and improved juggle properties

title.name: Sun Shot
title.notes: Sends a slower projectile forward a short distance that pauses before continuing forward; useful approach tool that can be followed up with Drive Rush pressure from midrange; can only have 1 meterless Sun Shot on-screen at a time (but can be used in conjunction with OD Sun Shot)

title.name: Sun Shot
title.notes: Sends a projectile forward a medium distance that pauses before continuing forward; can only have 1 meterless Sun Shot on-screen at a time (but can be used in conjunction with OD Sun Shot)

title.name: Sun Shot
title.notes: Uncharged version has 2f extra whiff recovery if the projectile does not connect within the first 40 frames; sends a projectile upward that pauses before either coming back down (uncharged) or continuing straight forward mid-air (charged); charged version is useful for baiting a whiffed parry or punishing opponents trying to jump out of Ingrid's projectile zoning; uncharged version can be used to safely stock a Sun Crest at mid range if the opponent doesn't jump over it; can only have 1 meterless Sun Shot on-screen at a time (but can be used in conjunction with OD Sun Shot)

title.name: Sun Shot (L/M)
title.notes: LP+MP gives the shorter/slower 236LP version (most useful for approaching from midrange); LP+MP gives the farther/faster 236MP version (more useful as a traditional zoning projectile); can only have 1 OD Sun Shot on-screen at a time (but can be used in conjunction with another meterless Sun Shot); SA2 cancel: KD+61/+10

title.name: Sun Shot (H)
title.notes: Uncharged version has 2f extra whiff recovery if the projectile does not connect within the first 35 frames; button combination determines projectile strength (LP+MP / LP+HP / MP+HP); sends a projectile upward that pauses before either coming back down (uncharged) or continuing straight forward mid-air (charged); charged version is useful for baiting a whiffed parry or punishing opponents trying to jump out of Ingrid's projectile zoning; uncharged version can be used to safely stock a Sun Crest at mid range if the opponent doesn't jump over it; can only have 1 OD Sun Shot on-screen at a time (but can be used in conjunction with another meterless Sun Shot); SA2 cancel: KD+61/+10

title.name: Sun Flare
title.notes: Builds 1 Sun Crest on frame 47; can hold the button to build up to 4 Sun Crests (stocks build slightly more quickly when held); held version builds Sun Crests on frames 42/88/134/182 (releasing the button early causes each stock level to be built 5f later); held version ends once 4 Sun Crests have been accumulated regardless of the initial stock count; button must be held for 23f (2-stock) / 85f (3-stock) / 131f (4-stock) respectively; if activated with 4 Sun Crests, Ingrid instead builds 3000 Drive gauge every 46 frames

title.name: Sun Flare
title.notes: With 0 Sun Crests, this version also comes out when input with HP

title.name: Sun Flare (1 Stock)
title.notes: Consumes 1 Sun Crest for more hits, extra damage, and longer range compared to the 0-stock MP/HP version

title.name: Sun Flare (2 Stock))
title.notes: Consumes 2 Sun Crests for more hits, extra damage, and longer range compared to the 1-stock HP version

title.name: Sun Flare
title.notes: Has the properties of the 1-stock HP version without spending any Sun Crests; SA2 cancel: KD+75/+11

title.name: Sun Flare (1 Stock)
title.notes: Has the properties of the 2-stock HP version but only costs 1 Sun Crest; has more hits, extra damage, and longer range compared to 0-stock OD version; SA2 cancel: KD+83/+12

title.name: Sun Flare (2 Stock)
title.notes: Has properties greater than the 2-stock HP version or 1-stock OD version for 2 Sun Crests; SA2 cancel: KD+94/+16

title.name: Solar Burst
title.notes: Can be used during Forward/Neutral jump only on frames 17-33; builds 1 Sun Crest on frame 47; the animation lasts 48f regardless of when it was input during the jump (more total recovery if input late in the jump arc); landing recovery ranges from 8-30f depending on activation time; Ingrid is in a counter-hit state for the entire duration and a Forced Knockdown state during all airborne frames; if performed with 4 Sun Crests, Ingrid builds 3000 Drive gauge instead; can be canceled into from 6HP

title.name: Solar Burst
title.notes: Can be used during Forward/Neutral jump only on frames 13-37; Ingrid will land before the projectile comes out if activated on jump frame 30-37; with 0 Sun Crests, this version also comes out when input with HP; can be canceled into from 6HP; puts opponents into a limited juggle state (corner follow-ups possible if input low to the ground); Ingrid is in a crouching state on the final landing recovery frame

title.name: Solar Burst (1 Stock)
title.notes: Can be used during Forward/Neutral jump only on frames 13-37; Ingrid will land before the projectile comes out if activated on jump frame 30-37; consumes 1 Sun Crest for more hits, extra damage, and longer range compared to 0-stock MP/HP version; can be canceled into from 6HP; puts opponents into a limited juggle state (corner follow-ups possible if input low to the ground); Ingrid is in a crouching state on the final landing recovery frame

title.name: Solar Burst (2 Stock)
title.notes: Can be used during Forward/Neutral jump only on frames 13-37; Ingrid will land before the projectile comes out if activated on jump frame 30-37; consumes 1 Sun Crest for more hits, extra damage, and longer range compared to 1-stock HP version; can be canceled into from 6HP; launches opponents upward into a limited juggle state; Ingrid is in a crouching state on the final landing recovery frame

title.name: Solar Burst
title.notes: Can be used during Forward/Neutral jump only on frames 13-37; Ingrid will land before the projectile comes out if activated on jump frame 30-37; has the properties of the 1-stock HP version without spending any Sun Crests; can be canceled into from 6HP; puts opponents into a limited juggle state (corner follow-ups possible if input low to the ground); Ingrid is in a crouching state on the final landing recovery frame

title.name: Solar Burst (1 Stock)
title.notes: Can be used during Forward/Neutral jump only on frames 13-37; Ingrid will land before the projectile comes out if activated on jump frame 30-37; has the properties of the 2-stock HP version but only costs 1 Sun Crest; can be canceled into from 6HP; launches opponents upward into a limited juggle state; Ingrid is in a crouching state on the final landing recovery frame

title.name: Solar Burst (2 Stock)
title.notes: Can be used during Forward/Neutral jump only on frames 13-37; Ingrid will land before the projectile comes out if activated on jump frame 30-37; has properties greater than the 2-stock HP version or 1-stock OD version for 2 Sun Crests; can be canceled into from 6HP; puts opponents into a free juggle OTG bounce state; if the opponent Perfect Parries and Ingrid lands before all 4 hits connect, all remaining hits will fail to come out resulting in worse frame advantage; Ingrid is in a crouching state on the final landing recovery frame

title.name: Sun Rise
title.notes: Strong anti-air but cannot hit cross-up; deals 200 less damage on active frames 4-10

title.name: Sun Rise
title.notes: 2 hits; Low Crush 12-20f (not airborne during these frames); combos from light normals

title.name: Sun Rise
title.notes: 4 hits; Low Crush 25-35f (not airborne during these frames); on hit, Ingrid recovers mid-air on frame 77 with the opponent in a limited juggle state (can follow up with j.MP knockdown / j.HP reset / j.214P)

title.name: Sun Rise
title.notes: 3 hits with a 4th hit occurring on hit only; Low Crush 14-26f (not airborne); safe buffer option that becomes +1 vs. Burnout; SA2 cancel: +15/+5

title.name: Sun Veil
title.notes: Counter that can absorb strikes (resulting in a counter-attack) or dissipate 1-hit non-Super projectiles; counter-attack is a strike knockdown that can be blocked if triggered by a low-recovery attack; absorbing a projectile results in 28f total recovery (often punishable if done from longer ranges); can be used to counter most strike-based Supers (useful option out of Drive Rush cancel on reaction to opponent's Super freeze)

title.name: Sun Veil
title.notes: Counter that can absorb strikes/throws (resulting in a counter-attack) or dissipate non-Super projectiles even with multiple hits; counter-attack is a strike knockdown that can be blocked if triggered by a safe jump (3f leniency on safe jump timing); after absorbing a projectile hit, Ingrid incurs up to 9f fully invincible recovery per hit (refreshing for each new hit absorbed) followed by 15f vulnerable recovery; can be used to counter most Supers (useful option out of Drive Rush cancel on reaction to opponent's Super freeze); Ingrid is +12 before opponent hits the ground but too far away for a juggle

title.name: Vanishing Sun (Forward)
title.notes: Teleports forward to the opponent's location and performs a kick that is safe on block; puts opponents into a limited juggle state (follow-ups possible on a corner airborne connect); can whiff in juggle routes where the opponent is flying backward too quickly

title.name: Vanishing Sun (Down)
title.notes: Teleports above the opponent and comes down with an overhead strike that is plus on block; puts opponents into a limited juggle state (follow-ups possible in the corner); puts airborne opponents into a limited juggle OTG bounce state; can whiff over opponents that are walking forward

title.name: Vanishing Sun (Back)
title.notes: Teleports away from the opponent; 22 vulnerable recovery frames after reappearing; Distance: 1.74

title.name: Shining Sun (0 Stock)
title.notes: Can hold the button to spend up to 2 Sun Crests (+400 damage per crest but reduced oki); Ingrid's primary reversal while in Burnout

title.name: Shining Sun (1 Stock)
title.notes: Hold the button for ? frames to spend 1 Sun Crest for 400 extra damage and a farther knockdown

title.name: Shining Sun (2 Stock)
title.notes: Hold the button for ? frames to spend 2 Sun Crests for 800 extra damage and a farther knockdown

title.name: Order of the Sun (0 Stock)
title.notes: Fires beacon into the air that spawns 3 downward-hitting projectiles; button strength determines beacon position (Modern Controls: hold back/neutral/forward to control position); LP version appears in front of the opponent, MP version appears above the opponent, HP version appears behind the opponent (except at fullscreen distance); can hold the button and spend 1 Sun Crest for an enhanced version that summons 2 additional projectiles; projectile startup varies based on strength: LP 103f / MP 68f / HP 98f

title.name: Order of the Sun (1 Stock)
title.notes: Fires beacon into the air that spawns 4 sets of downward-hitting projectiles; button strength determines beacon position (Modern Controls: hold back/neutral/forward to control position); LP version appears in front of the opponent, MP version appears above the opponent, HP version appears behind the opponent (except at fullscreen distance); consumes 1 Sun Crest in exchange for the additional projectiles; projectile startup varies based on strength: LP 103f / MP 68f / HP 98f

title.name: Order of the Sun (2 Stock)
title.notes: Fires beacon into the air that spawns 5 sets of downward-hitting projectiles; button strength determines beacon position (Modern Controls: hold back/neutral/forward to control position); LP version appears in front of the opponent, MP version appears above the opponent, HP version appears behind the opponent (except at fullscreen distance); consumes 2 Sun Crests in exchange for the additional projectiles; projectile startup varies based on strength: LP 103f / MP 68f / HP 98f

title.name: Cosmic Ray
title.notes: Projectile hitbox that targets the opponent's position (can be avoided if opponent is ascending, like jumping after the Super freeze); cinematic time regenerates ~2.1 Drive bars for Ingrid; very slow startup makes it a poor reversal

title.name: Cosmic Ray (CA)
title.notes: Projectile hitbox that targets the opponent's position (can be avoided if opponent is ascending, like jumping after the Super freeze); available at 25% HP or below; cinematic time regenerates ~2.3 Drive bars for Ingrid; very slow startup makes it a poor reversal

title.name: Neutral Taunt
title.notes: "Ahhh, time for a break I guess. Hmm? You too little one? Oh, you're such a little nice Monoid. Yes you are, yes you are, yes you are! See ya later!"

title.name: Forward Taunt
title.notes: "My power howls! Face me! Just kidding."; if opponent stands directly in front of Ingrid, she performs an attack that deals 0 damage but grants full Super gauge to the opponent on hit/block/parry; references M. Bison's back taunt

title.name: Back Taunt
title.notes: "Oop, I forgot I have an urgent matter. You, babysit this one for a while."; summons a controllable Monoid that cannot block and has access to 5 attacks; LP or LK is a divekick-like move that hits low, MP or MK is an overhead, HP or HK is a high strike, HP+HK performs a beam attack, and any button in the air performs an aerial strike; the Monoid is fully controllable mid-air; Ingrid comes back if the Monoid is hit or after not moving for ? frames; when Ingrid returns after standing idle and high fives the Monoid, input LP~LP~4~LK~HK (Modern: L~L~4~M~H) to absorb the Monoid and transform into a more powerful version ("Shin Ingrid"); this transformation also fully heals Ingrid, fills her Super gauge, and grants 4 Sun Crests; Shin Ingrid gains access to 2 new Hidden Art special moves (22PPP and 214214K) and a new SA2 (360+KK)

title.name: Down Taunt
title.notes: "You're done!"

title.name: Big Laser?
title.notes: Shin Ingrid only; tall 16-hit beam projectile that reaches fullscreen; expands forward during the first 4 active frames before reaching full range on the 5th active frame; most/all hits will connect even if opponent falls into the late active frames; chip damage deals 50% of its base damage rather than the usual 25%

title.name: Burnout Attack?
title.notes: Shin Ingrid only; immediately puts the opponent into Burnout on hit; cannot be canceled into from normals; hits twice on hit or once on block

title.name: Sun Octopus?
title.notes: Shin Ingrid only; command grab Super; opponent cannot jump after the freeze; can be comboed into on grounded opponents; cinematic time regenerates ~1.6 Drive bars for Ingrid

title.name: Monoid Low Dive
title.notes: Requires summoning the Monoid with Ingrid's Back Taunt; 3 hits (all hit low despite the divekick-like animation); can hold forward to steer and increase the attack's range; Modern Controls: L

title.name: Monoid Overhead Hammer
title.notes: Requires summoning the Monoid with Ingrid's Back Taunt; can hold forward to steer and increase the attack's range; knocks down on grounded opponents and resets airborne opponents; Modern Controls: M

title.name: Monoid Star Thrust
title.notes: Requires summoning the Monoid with Ingrid's Back Taunt; can hold forward to steer and increase the attack's range; knocks down on grounded opponents and resets airborne opponents; Modern Controls: H

title.name: Monoid Beam
title.notes: Requires summoning the Monoid with Ingrid's Back Taunt; cannot steer the attack forward like the other Monoid attacks; knocks down on both grounded and airborne opponents; input has no kara-cancel leniency (must hit HP+HK on the exact same frame); Modern Controls: Sp

title.name: Monoid Aerial Strike
title.notes: Requires summoning the Monoid with Ingrid's Back Taunt; can hold forward to steer and increase the attack's range; knocks down on grounded opponents and resets airborne opponents; gains a second hitbox on active frames 3-6 that can hit cross-up; Modern Controls: j.X

title.name: Monoid Super
title.notes: Slow 3-bar Super with low damage relative to its meter cost; unsafe on hit or block; on hit, Ingrid returns and high-fives the Monoid (can transform into Shin Ingrid using the LP~LP~4~LK~HK input before the high five, becoming much more unsafe on hit); Modern Controls: 214236H
